name: Asteria
description: Central nation on the continent of Thanor, born from the fragmentation of the Orelian Empire in 1722. A constitutional monarchy with a bicameral parliament, it spans diverse regions from the fertile Windward Plains to the isolated Shattered Isles.

name: Silvershield Basin
description: A fertile basin surrounding Lake Silvershield in the central highlands. Known for its orchards and vineyards, it serves as a breadbasket for the capital region.

name: Thornburg
description: A rugged mining town in the northern Thornwood region, established during the iron and copper boom of the 1840s. Its population is largely composed of guild miners and lumberjacks.

name: Marinersgate
description: Major coastal port on the Bay of Whispers, known for its cosmopolitan atmosphere and powerful merchant families. The city's harbor handles the bulk of Asteria's overseas trade.

name: Eastrun
description: A seasonal tributary of the Serpent River in the Eastern Reaches. It frequently floods during the spring thaw, sustaining sheep pastures and small irrigation channels.

name: Mount Vardun
description: A prominent granite peak in the central Spine of Aethon, visible from Silvershield City. Its scree slopes host rare alpine herbs sought by apothecaries.

name: Oldhaven
description: A historic coastal city on the Bay of Whispers, destroyed by a flood in 1823 and rebuilt as Newhaven before reverting to its original name after local protests in 1931. Known for its nautical museums and salt‑cured fish.

name: Demeter Valley
description: A narrow, fertile valley along the eastern border with Cerethia, claimed by both states since the Great Border War. It remains a militarized zone with scattered farms and a tense peace.

name: Havenport
description: Major port city on the Bay of Whispers, hub of trade and cosmopolitan culture. Founded during the early First Union period.

name: Serpent River
description: Longest river in the Eastern Reaches, draining the semi-arid plateau into the Sea of Whispers. Its basin is a key route for mineral transport.

name: Thornrun River
description: Major watercourse in the Thornwood, flowing from the northern highlands through taiga forests. Used for log driving by the Thornwood Lumber Commission.

name: Peak of the Spine
description: The highest summit in the Spine of Aethon range, overlooking Lake Silvershield. Its slopes are seasonally snow-capped and home to alpine shrines.

name: Windfield
description: Prosperous agricultural market town in the Windward Plains, known for its grain exchange and horse fairs. Grew rapidly during the Industrial Awakening.

name: Ironhold
description: Mining settlement in the Eastern Reaches, centered on iron and copper extraction. Established by the Royal Guild of Miners after the discovery of rich ore deposits.

name: Silvershield City
description: Administrative and cultural capital of Asteria, located on the shores of Lake Silvershield. Seat of the Grand Duke, parliament, and Silvershield University.

name: Southern Windward Plains
description: Subregion of the Windward Plains, characterized by gently rolling farmland and fortified manor houses. Historically a stronghold of rebellious local nobility.

name: Copperhold
description: A mining town in the central Thornwood, known for its copper and iron mines. It grew rapidly during the Industrial Awakening but has since declined due to resource depletion.

name: Windrun River
description: A major tributary of the Serpent River, flowing through the eastern Windward Plains. Its seasonal floods have shaped the agricultural patterns of the region.

name: The Beacon of Havenport
description: A leading daily newspaper based in Havenport, known for its liberal leanings and support of free trade. It was originally founded as The Harbor Herald and renamed in 1855.

name: Asterian National University
description: The premier higher education institution in Asteria, located in Silvershield City. It was founded during the Second Compact era and reorganized following the Great Border War.

name: Sturm & Sons Shipwrights
description: A family-owned shipbuilding company in Marinersgate, established in the late First Union period. It builds merchant and fishing vessels for the Bay of Whispers and the Shattered Isles.

name: The Regionalist Alliance
description: A political party formed in the Modernization Era advocating for greater autonomy for the Thornwood and Shattered Isles. It holds a small number of seats in the Elected Chamber.

name: The Astrian Royal Mint
description: The state institution responsible for minting all Astrian Crown coins and producing official seals. It was established alongside the Astrian National Bank in 1851.

name: The Eastern Reaches Chronicle
description: A small but influential newspaper based in Eastrun, focusing on mining news, border disputes with Cerethia, and local affairs. It was founded after the Great Border War.

name: Windward Grain Consortium
description: An agricultural cooperative controlling much of the grain trade in the Windward Plains. Founded by large estates, it has been criticized for price-fixing.

name: The Guild Free Trade Party
description: A pro-business political party that advocates dismantling guild licensing and removing internal trade barriers. It draws support from urban entrepreneurs and some rural manufacturers.

name: Silvershield University
description: Oldest university in Asteria, founded in 1789 on the shores of Lake Silvershield. Offers degrees in law, medicine, and natural philosophy.

name: Thornwood Lumber Commission
description: Semi-public corporation established in 1835 to regulate logging in the Thornwood region. Has a reputation for corruption and monopoly practices.

name: Royal Guild of Miners
description: Chartered guild that regulates all mining activities in the Eastern Reaches, controlling licenses and royalties since 1825.

name: Astrian National Bank
description: Central bank of Asteria, founded in 1851 to issue the Astrian Crown and manage monetary policy. Based in the capital.

name: Cerethian Front Institute
description: Think tank founded in 1920 to analyze and advise on border security and diplomatic relations with Cerethia.

name: Order of the Silver Key
description: Secretive society of archivists and historians dedicated to preserving ancient records and artifacts across Asteria. Exact founding date unknown.

name: Guild Unity Party
description: Political party advocating for the protection of traditional guild privileges and apprenticeship systems against free trade reforms. Formed in 1885.

name: Havenport Herald
description: Major daily newspaper based in Havenport, covering commerce, politics, and international news. Founded in 1840 during the Industrial Awakening.

name: Windward Plains Gazette
description: Daily newspaper based in Windfield, covering agricultural markets, local politics, and regional events. Founded during the Industrial Awakening, it has historically voiced the interests of the landed gentry.

name: Royal Astrian Geographical Survey
description: State-sponsored body founded to map Asteria's interior and assess mineral resources. Produced the first detailed maps of the Spine of Aethon and Eastern Reaches during the Second Compact period.

name: Alaric Thorwaldsen
description: Hereditary councilor from the Thornwood, known for opposing the Thornwood Lumber Commission's corruption. Led a brief secessionist movement in the 1970s.

name: Kaelina Marinsdotter
description: Prominent historian and archivist, member of the Order of the Silver Key. Authored a definitive study of the First Union period.

name: Darian Ironhold-Bergson
description: Master of the Royal Guild of Miners during the Industrial Awakening, known for expanding iron extraction in the Eastern Reaches and enforcing guild licensing.

name: Mira Starwind
description: Notorious smuggler operating out of the Shattered Isles, evaded border patrols for decades. Her network moved rare ores and Cerethian goods.

name: Torvin of Oldhaven
description: Leader of the failed 1856 republican coup, mockingly remembered on Stumble Day. After exile, he wrote pamphlets advocating for a republic.

name: Lyra Silvershield-Mere
description: Grand Duchess of Asteria from 1917 to 1935, oversaw post-war reconstruction and the expansion of suffrage to women. Her reign saw the creation of the Elected Chamber.

name: Fenris Hearth-Knutson
description: Master smith and inventor who pioneered the use of rare earths for steel alloys in the 1870s, boosting the Eastern Reaches' mining economy.

name: Isolde of Havenport
description: First female Grand Chamberlain (1949-1957), pushed through the repeal of outdated guild licensing laws and expanded trade with the Shattered Isles.

name: Aldric Feldwinson
description: A Windward Plains landowner who championed free trade and opposed guild monopolies during the Industrial Awakening.

name: Bjorn Thornsson
description: A Thornwood miner who rose to become a senator in the Elected Chamber, advocating for northern labor rights.

name: Lyra Tides-dotter
description: A Shattered Isles shipbuilder who modernized dockyards and expanded trade routes to the Cerethian border.

name: Orin Silvershield
description: A Silvershield University historian who wrote definitive works on Orelian-era folktales and the First Union.

name: Roric Redrock
description: An Eastern Reaches herder and smuggler who operated along the Serpent River during the border tensions.

name: Dorian Havenport
description: A Havenport banker who helped establish the Astrian National Bank's credit system during the Second Compact.

name: Thurston Thornwood
description: A corrupt commissioner of the Thornwood Lumber Commission who embezzled funds during the Industrial Awakening.

name: Lira Storm-dotter
description: A republican revolutionary from Silvershield City who died in the 1856 coup attempt, later memorialized in the banned third verse of the national anthem.

name: Eira Aldersdotter
description: Historian at Silvershield University, specializing in the Orelian Dominion period. Authored a critical study of the Second Compact's early years.

name: Gunnar Ironwood
description: Guild master of the Thornwood Lumber Commission in the late 19th century. Known for expanding logging operations into disputed northern tracts.

name: Seraphina Aldwyn
description: Member of the Elected Chamber from the Windward Plains. Advocated for land reform and reduction of guild privileges during the Post-War Restructuring.

name: Kaspar Tideson
description: Celebrated shipwright from the Shattered Isles, known for innovative hull designs. His work is credited with revitalizing the Isles' fishing fleet in the 1920s.

name: Freydis Eriksdotter
description: Republican activist from Havenport who participated in the 1856 coup attempt. Escaped execution and spent years in exile in Cerethia.

name: Lord Arvid of the Bay of Whispers
description: Influential noble of the Bay of Whispers region during the Second Compact. Opposed centralization and maintained private navies to enforce local maritime law.

attributes.title: Lord of the Bay of Whispers

name: Leif Coppersson
description: Self-taught inventor from Havenport during the Industrial Awakening. Designed early steam-powered canal boats and improved mining pumps, but struggled against guild licensing.

name: Sigrid Ash-dotter
aliases: Sister Sigrid of the Twisted Pine
description: Æther priestess in the Thornwood who preserved local animist traditions within the orthodox worship. Led a small monastic community near Mount Vardun.

name: Garrick Windfeld
description: Hereditary noble of the Windward Plains, owner of extensive grain estates. Served in the Hereditary Council and opposed centralizing reforms in the 1890s.

name: Torben Thornsson
description: Master of the Thornwood Lumber Commission, known for expanding logging operations into disputed northern territories. Involved in the commission's corruption scandals of the 1930s.

name: Finnian Tideson
description: Ship captain and smuggler operating from the Shattered Isles, running goods between Asteria and the eastern free ports. Wanted by the Astrian coast guard for customs evasion.

name: Brennus Iron-vein
description: Mining engineer and supervisor at the Copperhold mines, credited with introducing new shaft techniques that boosted rare earth extraction. Later served as a consultant to the Royal Guild of Miners.

name: Caspar Tidewell
description: Republican revolutionary who led the failed 1856 coup attempt. Proclaimed a short-lived 'People's Republic' before his arrest and execution. The event is mockingly called Stumble Day.

name: Elara Wind-dotter
description: Thornwood-born politician elected to the Elected Chamber in 1962. Championed the extension of universal suffrage and funding for northern infrastructure. Broke with the Regionalist Alliance over land rights.

name: Thornwood Freehold Movement
description: A political and cultural movement advocating for greater autonomy for the Thornwood region, arising from resentment of central taxation and guild monopolies on mining and lumber.

name: Astrian Steam-Loom
description: A mechanical weaving machine powered by steam, first installed in Havenport textile mills in 1838, dramatically increasing cloth production and fueling urban migration.

attributes.impact: Triggered the decline of hand-weaving guilds

name: Annals of the First Union
description: A comprehensive chronicle of the formation of the First Union, written by historian Caelum Thorwaldsen and published in 1805, widely used in secondary education.

name: The Windward Harvest
description: An oil painting from 1852 by artist Eldric Windfield, depicting an idealized wheat harvest on the Windward Plains; now displayed in the Grand Chamberlain’s residence.

name: Archipelec Fishermen's Syndicate
description: A cooperative of fishing families from the Shattered Isles, formed in 1870 to resist mainland merchant monopolies and preserve Archipelec maritime traditions.

name: Silvershield Electro-Plating Process
description: A method for depositing a thin layer of silver or copper onto metal objects using electric current, patented at Silvershield University in 1905 and used in coinage and luxury goods.

name: Myths of the Æther
description: A compilation of oral traditions and local saint tales associated with the Æther worship, first printed in 1840 by the Order of the Silver Key and later banned in the Thornwood for promoting heresy.

name: Stumble Day Monument
description: A bronze statue in Oldhaven depicting the rebel leader's horse stumbling on parliament steps, unveiled in 1900 as a satirical reminder of the 1856 coup attempt.

name: The Principles of Guild Governance
description: A treatise advocating for the reduction of hereditary privileges in guild governance, published during the Second Compact period.

name: People's Labor Party
description: A center-left party founded in 1966 to represent urban workers and small farmers, advocating for expanded welfare, guild reform, and regional decentralization.

name: Eastern Reaches Autonomy League
description: A political movement formed in 1972 demanding greater self-rule for the arid Eastern Reaches, citing neglect by the central government and cultural distinctiveness.

attributes.goals: regional parliament, control over mineral rights

name: Silvershield Environmental Commission
description: A government advisory body created in 1975 after the Serpent River sludge spill to monitor industrial pollution and recommend regulations.

name: Lord Elias Thornwood
description: Grand Chamberlain of Asteria from 1962 to 1974, known for overseeing the Universal Suffrage Act and navigating labor strikes through compromise. A moderate from the Hereditary Council.

name: Damaris Hawksworth
description: First female leader of the People's Labor Party, elected to the Elected Chamber in 1967. Championed the Seafarers' Welfare Act and miner safety reforms.

name: Serpent River Sludge Spill
description: A catastrophic release of toxic tailings from a mine upstream of Copperhold into the Serpent River in 1974, killing fish and sickening hundreds downstream. Led to the creation of the Silvershield Environmental Commission.

name: Universal Suffrage Act
description: Law passed in 1967 extending the franchise to all citizens aged 21 and over, regardless of gender, property, or guild membership. Doubled the electorate and led to the rise of the People's Labor Party.

name: Grand Convention of 1978
description: A failed attempt to rewrite the Second Compact (1856) to address regional autonomy, guild power, and the role of the monarchy. Convened in Silvershield City but dissolved without a final document when the Eastern Reaches Autonomy League walked out. Left constitutional questions unresolved.

name: Seafarers' Welfare Act of 1971
description: Landmark law providing accident insurance, minimum rations, and port health inspections for sailors in the Archipelec and Bay of Whispers. Sponsored by Damaris Hawksworth.

name: Eastern Reaches Regional Assembly
description: A devolved legislative body for the Eastern Reaches, established by the Autumn Accord of 1987. It has authority over local taxation and mineral revenue sharing, subject to Asterian parliamentary oversight.

name: Clean Waters Act
description: Landmark environmental legislation passed in 1990, imposing effluent limits on mining and lumber operations, creating the Silvershield Environmental Commission's enforcement powers, and establishing the Serpent River Basin Authority.

name: Copperhold Mine Safety Commission
description: An independent regulatory body created after the Copperhold Collapse (1995) to inspect and enforce safety standards in all guild-operated mines. Replaced the Royal Guild of Miners' self-regulation.

name: Third Compact
description: The constitutional document ratified in 2003 after the Conciliation Convention. It made regional governors elected, reduced the Hereditary Council's veto to a suspensive power, created an independent Supreme Court, and formalized the monarch's role as a non-partisan arbiter.

name: Astrian Banking Regulation Commission
description: Established in 1999 following the Crown devaluation crisis, it oversees the Astrian National Bank and private banks, sets reserve requirements, and manages the floating exchange rate introduced in 2001.

name: People's Ecologist Party
description: A green political party formed in 1991 by former members of the Silvershield Environmental Commission and activists from the Serpent River clean-up. Advocates for renewable energy, stricter pollution controls, and the break-up of guild monopolies.

name: Asteria First Movement
description: A nationalist-populist movement that emerged in the late 1990s, opposing the Third Compact's regional devolution and advocating for protectionist trade policies and a return to the original Second Compact powers. Gained support in rural and guild-dominated areas.

name: Æther-Link Telegraph Company
description: Founded in 1982 by engineers from Silvershield University, it pioneered wireless telegraphy using 'æther-wave' technology. Rapidly expanded across the Windward Plains and into the Eastern Reaches, becoming the dominant communications network by 1995.
